description: Lock Public Key Infrastructure to the 1988 X.509 certificate trust model and its constitutive public-key and digital-signature mechanisms.

checks.reason: X.509 originated in the international Directory standards; TCP/IP is a later transport and deployment context, not part of the certificate trust mechanism.
Software engineering is generic implementation background rather than a certificate-specific causal prerequisite.
X.509 certificates bind identities to public keys and path validation operates from trusted public keys.
Removing public-key cryptography would remove both the certified key and the trust-anchor validation mechanism.
Certification authorities sign certificates to certify each subject-to-public-key binding.
The CA signature is constitutive of the certificate model rather than an optional transport or deployment feature.
The scoped infrastructure begins with X.509 (11/88), approved on 25 November 1988.
